subject: [PATCH 2/6] nfsd: release path refs on follow_down() error

2026-05-31.patch_summary: This patch fixes a memory leak in the NFS daemon by releasing path references when follow_down() fails. The issue occurs because the error handling for follow_down() does not properly release the mntget() and dget() references taken on entry, leading to increased reference counts that can block unmounts and pin dentries against the shrinker. The patch adds a call to path_put(&path) before exiting the error handling branch in nfsd_cross_mnt(), ensuring that these references are properly released even when follow_down() fails.
